key_insight: HAMNO learns PDE solution operators by adaptively blending local convolution, global spectral context, and hierarchical processing, while PI-HAMNO adds physics-based losses to improve stability and accuracy.
engineering_relevance: This is useful for fast, mesh-aware surrogate modeling of nonlinear time-dependent mechanics and multiphysics systems where long-term rollout fidelity and physical consistency matter.
method: Adaptive gating between local and global multi-scale features
Physics-informed training with strong-form residual and weak-form FEM test-function constraints
evidence_signal: The method was evaluated on non-periodic Allen-Cahn, Cahn-Hilliard, and Swift-Hohenberg systems on cubic domains under long-horizon rollout, data-limited training, out-of-distribution initial conditions, and random-seed variation.
watch_item: Physics-informed gains may depend on accurate residual evaluation and quadrature/test-function setup, which can add implementation complexity and computational overhead.
paper_title: HAMNO: A Hierarchical Adaptive Multi-scale Neural Operator with Physics-Informed Learning for Dynamical Systems
